title: Enabling JavaScript support for WebViews is security-sensitive
description: Enabling JavaScript in WebViews can expose applications to security vulnerabilities, such as cross-site scripting (XSS) and code injection attacks. JavaScript should only be enabled if absolutely necessary, and all input should be properly validated and sanitized.
recommendation: Avoid enabling JavaScript in WebViews unless it is strictly required. If enabled, ensure that only trusted content is loaded and all user input is validated.
impact: Attackers may exploit enabled JavaScript in WebViews to execute malicious code, steal sensitive data, or compromise the application.

examples.bad:
WebView webView = ...;
webView.getSettings().setJavaScriptEnabled(true); // Unsafe if untrusted content is loaded

checks.message: Avoid enabling JavaScript in WebViews unless it is strictly required. If enabled, ensure that only trusted content is loaded and all user input is validated.
